title: The Categorical Imperative
description: The foundational principle of Immanuel Kant's deontological moral philosophy.
definition: A moral law that is unconditional or absolute for all agents, the validity or claim of which does not depend on any underlying purpose or desire. The primary formulation is: 'Act only according to that maxim whereby you can at the same time will that it should become a universal law.' It grounds morality in rational consistency and respect for persons as ends in themselves, not merely as means.

proofs.description: Developed in Kant's 'Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals' (1785).
Derived from the concept of a rational being and the nature of moral obligation.
